input.prompt:
You are test-agent for task T2: M1 - Auth Service + Controller + DTOs.

INPUT (Read these files FIRST):
- Task: Implement auth API layer — register, login, me endpoints with DTOs and service.
  ALLOWED files (5):
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/RegisterRequest.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/LoginRequest.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/LoginResponse.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/AuthService.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/AuthController.java

  Key requirements:
  - RegisterRequest: username (3-50, regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$), password (min 8), displayName (1-100). Jakarta validation.
  - LoginRequest: username, password (both required)
  - LoginResponse: token (String), expiresIn (long, value=86400)
  - AuthService: register(req) → {id, username, displayName}; login(req) → LoginResponse; getCurrentUser(userId) → {id, username, displayName}. BCrypt password hashing.
  - AuthController: POST /api/auth/register (201), POST /api/auth/login (200), GET /api/auth/me (200). For register and /me, return {id, username, displayName} directly.
  - Errors: 409 for duplicate username, 401 for bad credentials (no user enumeration), 400 for validation failures

  AC: M1-AC1 (register success 201), AC2 (duplicate 409), AC4 (login success 200 with token+expiresIn), AC5 (wrong password 401), AC6 (nonexistent user 401), AC7 (me returns user info), AC11 (username length), AC12 (password length), AC13 (displayName length), AC14 (username invalid chars)

- PLAN: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/2026-03-06-PLAN.md
- CONTRACT: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/2026-03-06-CONTRACT.md
- READONLY context (already implemented by T1, read to understand patterns):
  - C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/User.java
  - C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/repository/UserRepository.java
  - C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/security/JwtTokenProvider.java
  - C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/config/SecurityConfig.java
- Test directory: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/
- Log output: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/logs/test-agent-2.log.md

YOUR JOB: Design and write tests BEFORE any implementation code exists.

TEST DESIGN STRATEGY:
1. Red/Green Tests — from acceptance criteria (AC1-AC14 for M1)
2. Boundary Tests — username 2 chars, 51 chars; password 7 chars; displayName empty, 101 chars
3. Error Case Tests — 409, 401, 400 scenarios
4. Contract Tests — response schemas match CONTRACT

Write tests for:
- AuthController integration tests (@SpringBootTest + @AutoConfigureMockMvc) testing full HTTP request/response cycle
- AuthService unit tests (mock UserRepository + PasswordEncoder + JwtTokenProvider)

IMPORTANT: Read the existing T1 implementation files (User.java, UserRepository.java, JwtTokenProvider.java, SecurityConfig.java) to understand how to write tests that will work with the existing code. The T1 code is READONLY — your tests should be designed to work WITH it.

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES: Write to C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-2.md

Tech: Spring Boot 3.2.3, Java 17, NO Lombok, JUnit 5, MockMvc, @MockBean

You are test-agent for task T2: M1 - Auth Service + Controller + DTOs.

INPUT (Read these files FIRST):
- Task: Implement auth API layer — register, login, me endpoints with DTOs and service.
  ALLOWED files (5):
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/RegisterRequest.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/LoginRequest.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/LoginResponse.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/AuthService.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/AuthController.java

  Key requirements:
  - RegisterRequest: username (3-50, regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$), password (min 8), displayName (1-100). Jakarta validation.
  - LoginRequest: username, password (both required)
  - LoginResponse: token (String), expiresIn (long, value=86400)
  - AuthService: register(req) → {id, username, displayName}; login(req) → LoginResponse; getCurrentUser(userId) → {id, username, displayName}. BCrypt password hashing.
  - AuthController: POST /api/auth/register (201), POST /api/auth/login (200), GET /api/auth/me (200). For register and /me, return {id, username, displayName} directly.
  - Errors: 409 for duplicate username, 401 for bad credentials (no user enumeration), 400 for validation failures

  AC: M1-AC1 (register success 201), AC2 (duplicate 409), AC4 (login success 200 with token+expiresIn), AC5 (wrong password 401), AC6 (nonexistent user 401), AC7 (me returns user info), AC11 (username length), AC12 (password length), AC13 (displayName length), AC14 (username invalid chars)

input.prompt: You are test-agent for task T7: M6 - Frontend TaskListPage + Components. INPUT (Read these files FIRST): - Task: Implement all Vue components for the task list page. ALLOWED files (5): - frontend/src/views/TaskListPage.vue - frontend/src/components/TaskFilters.vue - frontend/src/components/TaskListItem.vue - frontend/src/components/TaskCreateForm.vue - frontend/src/components/Pagination.vue Key requirements: - TaskListPage.vue: main page at /tasks. Composes TaskFilters, TaskListItem list, TaskCreateForm (modal), Pagination. On mount calls taskStore.fetchTasks(). Loading indicator + error message. "New Task" button. "No tasks" empty state. - TaskFilters.vue: search input (keyword, debounce 300ms), priority dropdown (All/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), status dropdown (All/TODO/IN_PROGRESS/DONE/CLOSED). Uses taskStore actions. - TaskListItem.vue: props: task. Displays: title, status badge (TODO=#6B7280, IN_PROGRESS=#3B82F6, DONE=#10B981, CLOSED=#EF4444), priority badge (HIGH=#EF4444, MEDIUM=#F59E0B, LOW=#10B981), assignee displayName or "Not assigned", tags. <teammate-message teammate_id="test-agent-3" color="cyan" summary="T3 tests done: 5 files, 55 tests, impl notes written">
T3 test-agent complete. 5 test files, 55 test methods written.

## Test Files
1. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/entity/TaskTest.java` (20 tests)
   - Field access, defaults (priority=MEDIUM, status=TODO, deleted=false)
   - Priority enum (3 values), TaskStatus enum (4 values)
   - Title validation boundaries: null, empty, 1-char, 200-char, 201-char
   - Description validation: null ok, 5000-char, 5001-char

2. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/repository/TaskRepositoryTest.java` (17 tests)
   - CRUD: auto ID, auto createdAt, persist all fields, default priority/status
   - findByIdAndDeletedFalse: active found, soft-deleted excluded, nonexistent empty
   - findByFilters(keyword, priority, status, pageable): exclude soft-deleted, no filters, keyword case-insensitive, priority filter, status filter, combined filters, pagination, sorting, partial match, empty result
   - Standard findById still returns soft-deleted (verifies custom query necessity)

3. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskRequestTest.java` (11 tests)
   - Valid request, optional description/priority
   - Title/description validation boundaries

4. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskResponseTest.java` (4 tests)
   - All fields, nullable fields, CONTRACT field verification

5. `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskPageResponseTest.java` (3 tests)
   - All fields, empty content, CONTRACT structure

## Implementation Notes
Written to: `docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-3.md`

Key guidance for worker:
- Enums as inner classes of Task (Task.Priority, Task.TaskStatus)
- Repository method signature: `findByFilters(String keyword, Priority priority, TaskStatus status, Pageable pageable)` returning `Page<Task>`
- Use JPQL `(:param IS NULL OR t.field = :param)` pattern for optional filters
- `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(Long id)` as Spring Data derived query
- `@Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)` for both enums
- `@PrePersist` for createdAt auto-set

## AC Coverage
M2-AC1, AC2, AC3, AC8, AC10, AC11, AC12, AC15, AC16
</teammate-message>

input.prompt:
You are worker-2, member of team dev-taskflow-m1m2m3m6.
Superior: Team Lead (TL).
Allowed contacts: TL only.
Do NOT SendMessage anyone else. No peer communication.

YOUR TASK:
T2: M1 - Auth Service + Controller + DTOs

Implement the auth API layer — register, login, me endpoints with DTOs and service.

Acceptance criteria:
- RegisterRequest: username (3-50, regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$), password (min 8), displayName (1-100). Jakarta validation annotations.
- LoginRequest: username, password (both required)
- LoginResponse: token (String), expiresIn (long, value=86400)
- AuthService: register(req) → Map with {id, username, displayName}; login(req) → LoginResponse; getCurrentUser(userId) → Map with {id, username, displayName}. BCrypt password hashing via PasswordEncoder.
- AuthController: POST /api/auth/register (201), POST /api/auth/login (200), GET /api/auth/me (200). Return {id, username, displayName} for register and /me.
- Error: 409 for duplicate username, 401 for bad credentials (unified, BR7), 400 for validation
- Error response format: {"message": "..."} for all errors
- AC1-AC14 all covered by tests

File Scope:
- ALLOWED: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/RegisterRequest.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/LoginRequest.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/LoginResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/AuthService.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/AuthController.java
- READONLY: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/User.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/repository/UserRepository.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/security/JwtTokenProvider.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/config/SecurityConfig.java, backend/src/main/resources/application.yml
- Everything else: FORBIDDEN

4. Implementation notes: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-2.md
5. READONLY files listed above (especially User.java and JwtTokenProvider.java to understand APIs)

- Stay strictly within File Scope (5 ALLOWED files only)

input.prompt:
You are worker-3, member of team dev-taskflow-m1m2m3m6.
Superior: Team Lead (TL).

Do NOT SendMessage anyone else. No peer communication.

YOUR TASK:
T3: M2 - Task Entity + Repository + DTOs

Create Task entity, repository with custom queries, and request/response DTOs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Task entity: id (Long, auto-generated), title (1-200), description (max 5000), priority (enum HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW, default MEDIUM), status (enum TODO/IN_PROGRESS/DONE/CLOSED, default TODO), createdBy (Long — user id), createdAt (LocalDateTime, auto via @PrePersist), updatedAt (LocalDateTime, nullable), deleted (boolean, default false)
- Priority and TaskStatus as inner enums of Task (Task.Priority, Task.TaskStatus)
- @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING) for both enums
- TaskRepository: extends JpaRepository<Task, Long>
  - findByIdAndDeletedFalse(Long id) → Optional<Task> (Spring Data derived query)
  - @Query method: findByFilters(String keyword, Priority priority, TaskStatus status, Pageable pageable) → Page<Task>
    Use JPQL with (:param IS NULL OR t.field = :param) pattern for optional filters
    Must exclude deleted=true tasks
    Keyword search: LOWER(t.title) LIKE LOWER(CONCAT('%', :keyword, '%'))
- TaskRequest: title (required @NotBlank @Size(1,200)), description (optional @Size(max=5000)), priority (optional enum)
- TaskResponse: all entity fields as a simple POJO/record
- TaskPageResponse: content (List<TaskResponse>), page (int), size (int), totalElements (long), totalPages (int)

input.prompt:
You are worker-7, member of team dev-taskflow-m1m2m3m6.
Superior: Team Lead (TL).
Allowed contacts: TL only.
Do NOT SendMessage anyone else. No peer communication.

YOUR TASK:
T7: M6 - Frontend TaskListPage + Components

Implement all Vue components for the task list page.

Acceptance criteria:
- TaskListPage.vue: main page at /tasks. Composes TaskFilters, TaskListItem list, TaskCreateForm (modal), Pagination. On mount calls taskStore.fetchTasks(). Loading indicator + error message. "New Task" button toggles TaskCreateForm. Empty state: text matching /no task|empty|沒有任務/i.
- TaskFilters.vue: search input (keyword, debounce 300ms via setTimeout), priority dropdown (All/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), status dropdown (All/TODO/IN_PROGRESS/DONE/CLOSED). Uses taskStore.setKeyword(), setPriorityFilter(), setStatusFilter().
- TaskListItem.vue: props: task (Object). Displays: title, status badge with inline style color (TODO=#6B7280, IN_PROGRESS=#3B82F6, DONE=#10B981, CLOSED=#EF4444), priority badge with inline style color (HIGH=#EF4444, MEDIUM=#F59E0B, LOW=#10B981), assignee displayName or "Not assigned"/"未指派", tags. Emits click event on task click.
- TaskCreateForm.vue: props: visible (Boolean, v-if). Fields: title (required), description (optional), priority (dropdown, default MEDIUM). Emits: created (on successful create), cancel. Calls taskStore.createTask(data).
- Pagination.vue: props: currentPage (Number, 0-indexed), totalPages (Number). Emits: page-change(pageNumber). Prev/Next buttons. Disable prev when currentPage===0, disable next when currentPage>=totalPages-1. Show "page X of Y" (display as 1-indexed to user).

File Scope:
- ALLOWED: frontend/src/views/TaskListPage.vue, frontend/src/components/TaskFilters.vue, frontend/src/components/TaskListItem.vue, frontend/src/components/TaskCreateForm.vue, frontend/src/components/Pagination.vue
- READONLY: frontend/src/stores/taskStore.ts, frontend/src/api/tasks.ts, frontend/src/router/index.ts, frontend/vite.config.ts, frontend/package.json
- Everything else: FORBIDDEN

NOTE: TaskListPage.vue already exists as a minimal stub from T6. You MUST overwrite it with the full implementation.

REQUIRED READING (do this FIRST before writing any code):
1. PLAN: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/2026-03-06-PLAN.md

3. Test files: frontend/tests/views/TaskListPage.test.ts, frontend/tests/components/TaskFilters.test.ts, frontend/tests/components/TaskListItem.test.ts, frontend/tests/components/TaskCreateForm.test.ts, frontend/tests/components/Pagination.test.ts
4. Implementation notes: C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-7.md
5. READONLY files (especially taskStore.ts to understand store shape)

YOUR JOB:
- Write code that passes ALL 45 provided tests
- Stay strictly within File Scope (5 ALLOWED files only)
- Use Vue 3 Composition API with <script setup lang="ts">
- Badge colors: use inline :style="{ backgroundColor: ... }" or :style="{ color: ... }" — tests check exact hex codes in HTML

- npx vitest run tests/views tests/components --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tail -80

<teammate-message teammate_id="worker-2" color="blue" summary="T2 blocked: integration test missing JUnit import">
T2 implementation complete (5 files written). However, the integration test file `AuthControllerIntegrationTest.java` has a compilation error: it's missing `import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;` at the top. The unit test file `AuthServiceTest.java` has this import correctly (line 21), but the integration test file does not.

This causes 5 compilation errors for `assertTrue`, `assertNotEquals`, `assertEquals` used in nested classes.

The fix is adding one line to `AuthControllerIntegrationTest.java`:
```
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;

INPUT (Read these files FIRST):
- Task: Implement Task CRUD service and REST controller with pagination, search, and filter.

  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java

  Key requirements:
  - TaskService: createTask(userId, req), getTask(id), updateTask(id, req), deleteTask(id) — soft delete, listTasks(keyword, priority, status, pageable)
  - TaskController: POST /api/tasks (201), GET /api/tasks/{id} (200), PUT /api/tasks/{id} (200), DELETE /api/tasks/{id} (204), GET /api/tasks (200 with pagination)
  - Extract user ID from SecurityContext (JwtAuthenticationFilter sets UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken with principal = userId as Long)
  - Pagination: page=0, size=20, sort=createdAt,desc default
  - Query params: keyword, priority, status filters
  - Error: 404 for not found/soft-deleted, 400 for validation, 401 for unauthorized

2. TaskControllerIntegrationTest (@SpringBootTest + @AutoConfigureMockMvc): full HTTP tests.

IMPORTANT: Read AuthController.java and AuthService.java to understand the auth pattern — how to register/login and get a token for authenticated requests. input.prompt:
You are worker-4, member of team dev-taskflow-m1m2m3m6.
Superior: Team Lead (TL).
Allowed contacts: TL only.
Do NOT SendMessage anyone else. No peer communication.

YOUR TASK:
T4: M2 - Task Service + Controller

Implement Task CRUD service and REST controller with full pagination, search, and filter support.

Acceptance criteria (M2-AC1 through AC16):
- POST /api/tasks (201): create task, auto-set createdBy from JWT userId, default priority MEDIUM
- GET /api/tasks/{id} (200): return task, 404 if not found or soft-deleted
- PUT /api/tasks/{id} (200): update task, set updatedAt, 404 if not found
- DELETE /api/tasks/{id} (204): soft delete, no response body
- GET /api/tasks (200): paginated list, default page=0 size=20 sort=createdAt,desc. Supports keyword, priority, status filters
- Soft-deleted tasks excluded from list AND return 404 on direct access
- 401 for missing token, 400 for validation errors
- Error format: {"message": "..."}

File Scope:
- ALLOWED: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java
- READONLY: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/repository/TaskRepository.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskRequest.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskPageResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/security/JwtTokenProvider.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/config/SecurityConfig.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/AuthController.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/AuthService.java
- Everything else: FORBIDDEN

REQUIRED READING (do this FIRST):
1. PLAN: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-PLAN.md
2. CONTRACT: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-CONTRACT.md
3. Test files: backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskServiceTest.java, backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskControllerIntegrationTest.java
4. Implementation notes: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\temp\impl-notes-4.md
5. ALL READONLY files (especially Task.java, TaskRepository.java, DTOs, AuthController.java for patterns)

KEY IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Extract userId from SecurityContext: `((Long) SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication().getPrincipal())`
  (JwtAuthenticationFilter sets principal as Long userId)
- TaskService converts between Task entity and TaskResponse DTO
- TaskService.listTasks calls TaskRepository.findByFilters(keyword, priority, status, pageable)
- TaskService sets updatedAt on update, sets deleted=true on delete
- TaskController: use @Valid for request body validation
- Follow AuthController.java patterns for error handling

PARALLEL AWARENESS:
Worker-7 is working on frontend Vue components in parallel.
ONLY run YOUR test files:
- cd C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend
- ./mvnw test -Dtest="TaskServiceTest,TaskControllerIntegrationTest" -pl . 2>&1 | tail -80
Do NOT run the full test suite.

LOG WRITING:
Write to C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\logs\worker-4.log.md (append-only)

COMPLETION SEQUENCE:
1. Verify all 53 tests pass
1.5. Run `git diff --name-only` → compare against ALLOWED list. Revert out-of-scope.
2. Write final log entries
3. SendMessage TL: task ID, status, tests passed/failed, changed files list
4. Wait for TL response, then approve shutdown when requested

input.name: test-agent-5
input.prompt:
You are test-agent for task T5: M3 - Task State Machine.

INPUT (Read these files FIRST):
- Task: Implement state transition logic and add status change + transitions query endpoints.
  ALLOWED files (4):
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachine.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/StatusChangeRequest.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TransitionResponse.java
  - backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java (modify — add 2 new endpoints)

  Key requirements:
  - TaskStateMachine: valid transitions map:
    TODO → [IN_PROGRESS]
    IN_PROGRESS → [TODO, DONE]
    DONE → [CLOSED]
    CLOSED → [] (terminal)
    Methods: canTransition(from, to), getAvailableTransitions(status), transition(taskId, targetStatus)
  - StatusChangeRequest: status (enum TaskStatus, required)
  - TransitionResponse: currentStatus (String), availableTransitions (List<String>)
  - PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status (200, returns TaskResponse): validate transition, update status + updatedAt
  - GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions (200, returns TransitionResponse)

<teammate-message teammate_id="worker-7" color="yellow" summary="T7: 47/49 pass, 2 fail due to test pinia bug">
T7 status: 47/49 tests pass, 2 fail due to a test design bug.

**Passing**: All 40 component tests (TaskFilters 5/5, TaskListItem 15/15, TaskCreateForm 7/7 (actually 8 in suite), Pagination 10/10) + 7/9 TaskListPage tests.

**Failing (test bug)**:
1. `should show loading indicator when loading`
2. `should show error message when API fails`

**Root cause**: `mountPage()` in `TaskListPage.test.ts` passes `plugins: [createPinia()]` which creates a SECOND pinia instance (pinia_B). The component's `useTaskStore()` uses pinia_B (via Vue's inject). The test sets `store.loading = true` / `store.error = 'Network error'` on the FIRST pinia (pinia_A, from `setActivePinia` in `beforeEach`). Since these are separate pinia instances, the component never sees the test's store state.

I verified this empirically with debug tests: mounting without `plugins: [createPinia()]` correctly shares state; mounting with it creates isolated stores.

**Fix needed in test**: Remove `plugins: [createPinia()]` from `mountPage()` — the globally active pinia from `setActivePinia` is sufficient.

**Changed files** (all within ALLOWED scope):
- `frontend/src/views/TaskListPage.vue` (full implementation)
- `frontend/src/components/TaskFilters.vue` (new)
- `frontend/src/components/TaskListItem.vue` (new)
- `frontend/src/components/TaskCreateForm.vue` (new)
- `frontend/src/components/Pagination.vue` (new)

Please advise: should I wait for a test fix, or submit as-is (47/49)?
</teammate-message>

<teammate-message teammate_id="test-agent-5" color="blue" summary="T5 tests done: 37 tests, all 16 ACs covered">
T5 test-agent complete. Wrote 37 tests (17 unit + 20 integration) covering all M3-AC1 through AC16.

Files:
- `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachineTest.java` — 17 unit tests for canTransition(), getAvailableTransitions(), terminal state
- `backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/StatusTransitionIntegrationTest.java` — 20 integration tests: valid transitions (AC2-5), invalid transitions with 400 + "Cannot transition" message (AC6-9), updatedAt (AC10), GET transitions per state (AC11-14), 401 no token (AC15), 404 not found (AC16), response shape
- `docs/dev-team/2026-03-06-180000/temp/impl-notes-5.md` — 12 implementation notes for worker

Key impl-notes for worker:
- No Lombok (manual getters/setters)
- TaskStateMachine should be instantiable with `new TaskStateMachine()` (no-arg constructor for unit tests)
- Expected methods: `canTransition(from, to)` -> boolean, `getAvailableTransitions(status)` -> List<TaskStatus>
- Error message format: "Cannot transition from X to Y"
- Use `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id)` for task lookup
- Set `updatedAt` manually (no @PreUpdate)
- TransitionResponse.availableTransitions is List<String> (use status.name())
- PATCH returns TaskResponse, GET returns TransitionResponse
</teammate-message>

input.prompt:
You are worker-5, member of team dev-taskflow-m1m2m3m6.
Superior: Team Lead (TL).
Allowed contacts: TL only.
Do NOT SendMessage anyone else. No peer communication.

YOUR TASK:
T5: M3 - Task State Machine

Implement state transition logic and add status change + transitions query endpoints to TaskController.

Acceptance criteria (M3-AC1-16):
- TaskStateMachine: valid transitions: TODO→[IN_PROGRESS], IN_PROGRESS→[TODO,DONE], DONE→[CLOSED], CLOSED→[] (terminal)
- Methods: canTransition(from, to) → boolean, getAvailableTransitions(status) → List<TaskStatus>
- PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status: validate transition, update status + updatedAt, return TaskResponse (200). Error 400 "Cannot transition from X to Y"
- GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions: return TransitionResponse {currentStatus, availableTransitions} (200)
- StatusChangeRequest: status (enum TaskStatus, required)
- TransitionResponse: currentStatus (String), availableTransitions (List<String>)
- 401 without token, 404 for nonexistent task

File Scope:
- ALLOWED: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachine.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/StatusChangeRequest.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TransitionResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java (modify — add 2 new endpoints)
- READONLY: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/repository/TaskRepository.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/AuthController.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/AuthService.java
- Everything else: FORBIDDEN

REQUIRED READING (do this FIRST):
1. PLAN: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-PLAN.md
2. CONTRACT: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-CONTRACT.md
3. Test files: backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachineTest.java, backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/StatusTransitionIntegrationTest.java
4. Implementation notes: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\temp\impl-notes-5.md
5. ALL READONLY files (especially TaskController.java to understand existing structure, Task.java for enums, TaskService.java for patterns)

KEY DETAILS:
- TaskStateMachine must have no-arg constructor (unit tests instantiate with `new TaskStateMachine()`)
- Make it a @Component so it can be injected into TaskController
- Error message: "Cannot transition from {FROM} to {TO}"
- Use TaskRepository.findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id) for task lookup
- Set task.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now()) on successful transition
- TransitionResponse.availableTransitions uses status.name() for String conversion
- PATCH returns TaskResponse (reuse existing DTO conversion pattern from TaskService)
- Add endpoints to EXISTING TaskController — do NOT create a new controller

PARALLEL AWARENESS:
No other workers running. You are the last implementation task.
Run YOUR test files:
- cd C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend
- ./mvnw test -Dtest="TaskStateMachineTest,StatusTransitionIntegrationTest" -pl . 2>&1 | tail -80

LOG WRITING:
Write to C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\logs\worker-5.log.md (append-only)

COMPLETION SEQUENCE:
1. Verify all 37 tests pass
1.5. Run `git diff --name-only` → compare against ALLOWED list. Revert out-of-scope.
2. Write final log entries
3. SendMessage TL: task ID, status, tests passed/failed, changed files list
4. Wait for TL response, then approve shutdown when requested

Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
   - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
   - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
7. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
8. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
9. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>

</analysis>

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:

1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked the `dev-team:dev-team` skill with argument "實作M1+M2+M3+M6" to implement 4 modules of the TaskFlow application:
   - **M1**: JWT Authentication (register, login, me endpoints)
   - **M2**: Task CRUD (create, read, update, soft-delete, paginated list with filters)
   - **M3**: Task State Machine (TODO→IN_PROGRESS↔TODO, IN_PROGRESS→DONE→CLOSED)
   - **M6**: Task List Page (Vue 3 frontend with filters, pagination, task creation)
   
   The user prepared a git branch `experiment/E2-v1.4.0` with a skeleton project and confirmed all planning decisions.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Dev-team skill v1.4.0**: Phase 0-4 workflow (Plan → Contract → Execute → Global Review → Delivery)
   - **Spring Boot 3.2.3** + Java 17 + H2 in-memory DB + JJWT 0.12.5 + Spring Security 6.x
   - **Vue 3** + Pinia state management + axios HTTP client + vue-router + Vite 5 + Vitest
   - **TDD approach**: test-agent writes tests first (RED), worker implements (GREEN), QA reviews (three-way verification)
   - **JWT authentication**: BCrypt password hashing, Bearer token, 24h expiry, OncePerRequestFilter
   - **Soft delete pattern**: `deleted` boolean field, repository queries auto-exclude
   - **Task state machine**: EnumMap-based transitions with terminal state (CLOSED)
   - **Cross-cutting concerns**: unified error format `{"message":"..."}`, JWT filter, validation via Jakarta annotations, axios interceptor
   - **Parallel execution**: ≤3 concurrent workers, wave-based dependency resolution

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - **AuthControllerIntegrationTest.java missing import**:
     - Error: test-agent-2 wrote the test file without `import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;`, causing 5 compilation errors for assertTrue/assertNotEquals/assertEquals
     - Fix: TL added the missing import line after `import static org.hamcrest.Matchers.*;`
     - Worker-2 was notified and re-ran tests successfully
   
   - **TaskListPage.test.ts double pinia instance**:
     - Error: `mountPage()` function included `plugins: [createPinia()]` which created a SECOND pinia instance. Tests set state on the first pinia (from `setActivePinia` in `beforeEach`), but the component used the second pinia, so loading/error state was never visible.
     - Fix: TL removed `plugins: [createPinia()]` from `mountPage()` function
     - Worker-7 was notified and all 49/49 tests passed after fix

   - **Worker-3 temporarily renamed AuthControllerIntegrationTest.java**:
     - Worker-3 had to temporarily rename the broken test file to `.bak` to compile and run its own tests, then restored it. This was caused by the same missing import issue.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Dependency management**: Tasks were split into 7 pieces respecting the ≤5 ALLOWED files rule, with correct blockedBy chains (T1→T2, T1→T3, T2+T3→T4, T4→T5, T6→T7)
   - **Parallelism optimization**: Wave-based execution with up to 3 concurrent workers, QA and test-agents running in parallel with development
   - **Cross-cutting concerns**: Identified and documented in PLAN (error format, JWT filter, soft delete, validation, axios interceptor) so all workers follow unified patterns
   - **Test-agent bugs**: TL directly fixed 2 test file bugs rather than spawning fix tasks, saving time
